questions.question: Consider a binary classification problem with two features X1 and X2, which are continuous attributes. The prior probabilities of the classes are P(C1) = 0.6 and P(C2) = 0.4. Calculate the probability of class C1 given the feature values X1 = 5 and X2 = 10. Use the Naïve Bayes algorithm. Show your working.

A dataset is divided into two classes, A and B, with two continuous attributes X1 and X2. The class-conditional probability density functions are Gaussian with means μ1 = 2, σ1 = 1 for class A and μ2 = 5, σ2 = 2 for class B. Calculate the posterior probability of class A given the feature values X1 = 6 and X2 = 8. Use the Naïve Bayes algorithm. Show your working.

A Naïve Bayes classifier is trained on a dataset with three features X1, X2, and X3, which are continuous attributes. The class-conditional probability density functions are Gaussian with means μ1 = 1, μ2 = 7, and μ3 = 3, and standard deviations σ1 = 0.5, σ2 = 0.8, and σ3 = 0.4. Calculate the probability of class C1 given the feature values X1 = 0.6, X2 = 0.3, and X3 = 0.9. Use the Naïve Bayes algorithm. Show your working.
